BootstrapIngest.description: Batch of bootstrap daily aggregates from one machine.
BootstrapIngest.title: BootstrapIngest

ChannelConfigIn.description: Editable channel fields; absent field = unchanged, "" = clear to env.
ChannelConfigIn.title: ChannelConfigIn

ChannelFieldOut.description: One channel config field as shown in the UI (secrets masked).
ChannelFieldOut.title: ChannelFieldOut

ChannelOut.description: A notification channel's configured state and (masked) fields.
ChannelOut.title: ChannelOut

CostRowOut.description: One grouped cost aggregate: dual metrics never merged (FR-COST-012).
CostRowOut.title: CostRowOut

DailyAggregateIn.description: One bootstrap daily aggregate as sent by a collector.
DailyAggregateIn.title: DailyAggregateIn

DataQualityResponse.description: A keyset-paginated page of data-quality events.
DataQualityResponse.title: DataQualityResponse

EvaluateResult.description: Summary of a manual evaluation run.
EvaluateResult.title: EvaluateResult

EventKind.description:
What a v2 event record represents (FR-EVENT-003).

ATTEMPT: one upstream provider attempt -- the only billable kind
    (FR-EVENT-004).
LOGICAL_REQUEST: a summary grouping the attempts of one logical request.
IMPORT: a record brought in from an external dataset or backfill.
ADJUSTMENT: an administrative correction to earlier usage.
EventKind.title: EventKind

EventsIngest.description: Batch of usage events from one machine.
EventsIngest.title: EventsIngest

Finality.description:
Whether an event is an in-progress snapshot or a terminal state.

SNAPSHOT: a partial, still-changing view (for example a streamed response
    mid-flight); superseded by a higher sequence or a final event.
FINAL: the terminal state; supersedes any snapshot and only changes again
    through an explicit correction (FR-IDEMP-004/005).
Finality.title: Finality

HeatmapResponse.description: Calendar (daily) and punch-card (weekday x hour) usage.
HeatmapResponse.title: HeatmapResponse

ImportChangeOut.description: One row's effect in an import diff (new/superseded/unchanged/conflict).
ImportChangeOut.title: ImportChangeOut

ImportRequest.description: Apply a rate-card import; ``digest`` is required to apply a dry run.
ImportRequest.title: ImportRequest

ImportResponse.description: A rate-card import dry-run diff or apply result (D-015).
ImportResponse.title: ImportResponse

LimitForecastOut.description: An exhaustion forecast for one limit stream with confidence (FR-LIMIT-008).
LimitForecastOut.title: LimitForecastOut

LimitForecastResponse.description: Per-stream limit exhaustion forecasts over a range.
LimitForecastResponse.title: LimitForecastResponse

LimitSnapshotIn.description: One limit-window snapshot as sent by a collector.
LimitSnapshotIn.title: LimitSnapshotIn

LimitSnapshotOut.description:
One limit-utilization snapshot with its provenance (FR-LIMIT-004).

The v2 dimensions (account/organization/source_id) and measures
(limit_amount/remaining/unit) are null for v1 and dimension-less snapshots.
LimitSnapshotOut.title: LimitSnapshotOut

LimitSnapshotV2.description:
A provider-neutral rate-limit window snapshot (FR-LIMIT-002..004).

``window_kind`` is an opaque, provider-defined label (Anthropic's
``five_hour``, a Codex window, a Z.ai coding-plan window), so new providers
need no schema change. The extended dimensions -- ``account``,
``organization``, ``source``, ``remaining``, ``limit_amount``, ``unit`` --
are the v2 additions over v1's utilization-only snapshot; the dedicated
``limit_snapshots`` columns for them land in Task 69, so until then the
ingest endpoint preserves them in the row's ``raw`` JSON. Snapshots are
append-only.
LimitSnapshotV2.title: LimitSnapshotV2

LimitStreamOut.description: The stream a forecast was computed from (its source data, FR-LIMIT-008).
LimitStreamOut.title: LimitStreamOut

LimitsIngest.description: Batch of limit snapshots from one machine.
LimitsIngest.title: LimitsIngest

LimitsResponse.description: A keyset-paginated page of limit snapshots.
LimitsResponse.title: LimitsResponse

MachineInfo.description: Identifying information for the reporting machine.
MachineInfo.title: MachineInfo

MetaIngestResponse.description: Result of the v2 limits and aggregates ingest endpoints.
MetaIngestResponse.title: MetaIngestResponse

ModelOut.description:
Registry metadata for one model, with its alias spellings (FR-MODEL-010).

``native_model_id`` is the provider's own id; ``aliases`` are the alternate
spellings that normalize to it, so consumers see both the native and the
normalized forms where both exist.
ModelOut.title: ModelOut

OverviewOut.description: All-time totals for the dashboard summary strip.
OverviewOut.title: OverviewOut

PredictionOut.description: Extrapolated exhaustion for a limit window.
PredictionOut.title: PredictionOut

PriceRowIn.description: A price row to create or override.
PriceRowIn.title: PriceRowIn

Provenance.description:
Origin of a number, so the UI can label official vs estimated data.

OFFICIAL: read from a provider's authoritative endpoint.
LOCAL_ESTIMATE: derived from local artifacts (for example transcripts).
STATS_CACHE: imported once from a provider's local aggregate cache.
IMPORTED: brought in from an external dataset or backfill import.
ADJUSTED: written by an explicit administrative correction.

``IMPORTED`` and ``ADJUSTED`` are required by the v2 event model
(FR-EVENT-025); the first three predate it and keep their v1 meaning.
Provenance.title: Provenance

PunchCell.description: One weekday/hour cell of the punch card.
PunchCell.title: PunchCell

QueryWarningOut.description: A data-quality caveat attached to a v2 query response (FR-QUERY-010).
QueryWarningOut.title: QueryWarningOut

RateCardCloseRequest.description: Close a rate card by setting its effective_to date.
RateCardCloseRequest.title: RateCardCloseRequest

RateCardCloseResponse.description: The outcome of closing a rate card.
RateCardCloseResponse.title: RateCardCloseResponse

RateCardCreateRequest.description: Create a rate card (manual price or override).
RateCardCreateRequest.title: RateCardCreateRequest

RateCardMutationResponse.description: A created rate card plus the current pricing-state version.
RateCardMutationResponse.title: RateCardMutationResponse

RateCardOut.description: One stored rate card (the v2 per-unit pricing grain).
RateCardOut.title: RateCardOut

RebuildResult.description: Outcome of a full rollup rebuild (project regrouping).
RebuildResult.title: RebuildResult

ReconciliationRowOut.description: Observed-versus-computed cost drift for a provider (and optional day).
ReconciliationRowOut.title: ReconciliationRowOut

ReportOut.description: The full optimization report over a range.
ReportOut.title: ReportOut

RepriceRequest.description: Reprice a time range's costs under a new pricing version.
RepriceRequest.title: RepriceRequest

RepriceResponse.description: The outcome of a reprice or revert operation.

RequestDetailResponse.description: A logical request with its ordered attempts (fallback-chain drilldown).
RequestDetailResponse.title: RequestDetailResponse

RequestOut.description: A logical request with its attempt-chain aggregates (FR-TRACE-007/012).
RequestOut.title: RequestOut

RequestsResponse.description: A keyset-paginated page of logical requests.
RequestsResponse.title: RequestsResponse

RevertRequest.description: Re-activate a named prior pricing version for a time range.
RevertRequest.title: RevertRequest

RollupOut.description: One daily_rollups row exposed for external tooling (stable columns).
RollupOut.title: RollupOut

RollupsResponse.description: A keyset-paginated page of daily rollup rows.
RollupsResponse.title: RollupsResponse

Routing.description:
Gateway-neutral routing metadata for one attempt (FR-EVENT-019).

Optional and provider-agnostic: a gateway that cascades across models
records the policy that chose a model, why it was chosen, this attempt's
index in the chain, and -- for a fallback -- which model it fell back from
and what triggered the fallback. Gateway-specific detail beyond these
fields belongs in ``UsageEventV2.extra`` under a namespaced key.
Routing.title: Routing

SessionDetailOut.description: A session's ordered event series and derived stats.
SessionDetailOut.title: SessionDetailOut

SessionEventOut.description: One usage event in a session drill-down (metadata only).
SessionEventOut.title: SessionEventOut

SessionStatsOut.description: Derived efficiency signals for a session.
SessionStatsOut.title: SessionStatsOut

SessionsResponse.description: A paginated page of session rollups.
SessionsResponse.title: SessionsResponse

SourceHealthOut.description: Query-time health of a reporting source (FR-SOURCE-005).
SourceHealthOut.title: SourceHealthOut

SourceOut.description: A reporting source with its health (FR-SOURCE-001..006). No secrets.
SourceOut.title: SourceOut

SourceRef.description:
Identity of the source that reported an event (FR-EVENT-018).

Source identity is distinct from machine identity (FR-SOURCE-003): one
machine may host several sources. ``version`` is required so source health
can track schema/version drift; ``instance_id`` distinguishes concurrent
instances of the same named source and is optional.
SourceRef.title: SourceRef

SourceType.description: The kind of reporting source a v2 event came from (FR-SOURCE-002).
SourceType.title: SourceType

SourceUpdateRequest.description: Mutable source fields (label and billing mode); event identity is fixed.
SourceUpdateRequest.title: SourceUpdateRequest

SyncResult.description: Outcome of a LiteLLM price sync.
SyncResult.title: SyncResult

TestChannelResult.description: Outcome of a test notification to one channel.
TestChannelResult.title: TestChannelResult

TokenCreateRequest.description:
Request to mint an API token.

``scopes`` default to the full set when omitted (compatibility with
pre-scope clients); ``source_allowlist`` optionally restricts which
reporting sources the token may ingest for.
TokenCreateRequest.title: TokenCreateRequest

TokenCreatedOut.description: A newly created token; the secret appears only here.
TokenCreatedOut.title: TokenCreatedOut

UnknownModelReportRow.description: An unknown-model observation recorded at ingest.
UnknownModelReportRow.title: UnknownModelReportRow

UnpricedReportRow.description: An aggregate of unpriced or partially priced events for one model.
UnpricedReportRow.title: UnpricedReportRow

UsageBucketOut.description: Aggregated usage for one group-by key.
UsageBucketOut.title: UsageBucketOut

UsageEventIn.description: One usage event as sent by a collector.
UsageEventIn.title: UsageEventIn

UsageEventV2.description:
One provider-neutral v2 usage event (PRD Section 12.3 wire shape).

``event_id`` is unique within the canonical provider namespace
(FR-EVENT-002); ingest resolves revisions of the same id by ``finality``
and ``sequence``. Token counters are non-negative and default to zero so a
failed or cancelled attempt is ingestible with no usage (FR-EVENT-024).
``reasoning_tokens`` is stored apart from ``output_tokens`` (FR-EVENT-015),
and ``success`` (did the attempt succeed) is kept separate from ``outcome``
(the nuanced terminal state) per FR-EVENT-017.
UsageEventV2.title: UsageEventV2

UsageRowOut.description: One grouped usage aggregate (six counters plus attempt count).
UsageRowOut.title: UsageRowOut

ValidationErrorItem.description:
One structured validation failure (FR-INGEST-006).

``index`` is the event's position in the batch (``-1`` for a batch-envelope
error); ``field_path`` is a dotted path into the event; ``code`` and
``message`` name and describe the failure. This is the stable shape the
generated clients (task 62.12) and the exporter conformance suite consume.
ValidationErrorItem.title: ValidationErrorItem

WindowOut.description: One limit-window descriptor from the provider registry (FR-LIMIT-012).
WindowOut.title: WindowOut

tokemetry_server__api__schemas_query__Co.description: Cost over a range with the subscription value multiple.
tokemetry_server__api__schemas_query__Co.title: CostResponse

tokemetry_server__api__v2__schemas__Sess.description: A session rollup keyed by scoped identity (FR-TRACE-010/011).
tokemetry_server__api__v2__schemas__Sess.title: SessionOut

tokemetry_server__api__v2__schemas__Usag.description: Grouped final-attempt usage with data-quality warnings (FR-QUERY-007).
tokemetry_server__api__v2__schemas__Usag.title: UsageResponse

info.description: Two API versions coexist during the provider-neutral migration (FR-INGEST-011/014). **v1** (`/api/v1/*`) is the stable collector surface and stays wire-compatible for the whole program. **v2** (`/api/v2/*`) is the provider-neutral surface: batch ingest (`/ingest/events|limits|aggregates`), pre-flight `validate`, the published usage-event JSON schema (`/schemas/usage-event`), and read-only registries. Version is selected by URL path (there is no content negotiation); clients pin a major version and a bearer token, and every response carries an `X-Request-ID`.
info.summary: Self-hosted multi-machine AI token usage tracking
info.title: tokemetry

post.description:
Delete derived rollups and rebuild them, re-applying project grouping.

Run after changing the project-grouping configuration so historical
breakdowns regroup (worktrees and case-variant paths fold together).

get.description: Return the pricing table.

schema.title: Response Pricing Api V1 Pricing Get

post.description: Create or override a price row (upsert on provider/model/date).

Reprice all events and rollups against the current price table.

Also hot-swaps the ingest cost function so subsequent ingests use the
updated prices without a restart.

Fetch LiteLLM's price database and upsert Anthropic rows.

Prices are stored with an early effective date so they apply to all
historical events (equivalent-cost estimation for a subscriber, where
prices rarely change). Does not recompute stored costs; call
``/recompute`` afterward.

For v2 compatibility this also feeds the ``rate_cards`` table by running the
v2 import (dry run plus immediate auto-apply, labeled ``v1_sync`` in the
audit log), so the legacy endpoint keeps both pricing stacks current.

get.description: Return the token-optimization report (scorecard + ranked recommendations).

Return the optimization report as an LLM-ready Markdown download.

The document embeds an analysis prompt, a data dictionary, and the report
tables so it can be pasted into any AI agent. ``size=full`` additionally
embeds the top sessions and detected anomalies.
Return recent sessions, newest first.

schema.title: Response Sessions Api V1 Sessions Get

post.description: Create a rate card (manual price or override); rejects date overlaps.

Diff (``dry_run=true``) or apply a LiteLLM + curated rate-card import.

A dry run returns the structured diff and a digest without persisting. To
apply, call again with ``dry_run=false`` and the dry run's ``digest``; the
apply is rejected (409) if the stored rates changed since the dry run.
